Patta Barawari
Family Home

Architect Brief & Design Direction

A practical, warm, timeless family home for Patta Barawari, Solan. The house should be designed around parents' daily use on the ground floor, with family bedrooms on the first floor and a partial third floor for guests + terrace.

LocationPatta Barawari, Solan
Built-up3,500–4,000 sq ft
Outdoor~1,000 sq ft garden / verandah / terrace
Budget₹75 lakh–₹1 Cr all-inclusive
Primary instruction: Use the cream/ivory villa reference as the exterior language, but adapt it into a clear 3-level Himachal family home. Do not make carport/garage the visual focus.

Pantry access note: The pantry/store should open directly from the kitchen zone, not from the sitting area or staircase side.

1. Primary Exterior Direction

How to adapt this image

  • Convert to a clear 3-level house: Ground + First + Partial Third Floor.
  • Third floor should include 2 guest bedrooms, 2 attached bathrooms and a large terrace.
  • Use cream / ivory plaster walls, not dark chalet style.
  • Add darker sloping roof suitable for Himachal weather.
  • Add deeper verandahs and roof overhangs for monsoon protection.
  • Use limited local Himachal stone at plinth, entrance, chimney or landscape walls.
  • Large windows are okay, but avoid glass walls and resort-style glazing.
Remove completely: old chalet-style design references, excessive glazing, double-height glass walls, complex roof intersections and large carport frontage.

2. How The House Will Actually Be Used

Daily / regular use

  • Parents will primarily use the ground floor throughout the year.
  • Traditional Himachali kitchen will be the main daily-use space.
  • Modern support kitchen will sit adjacent to the traditional kitchen.
  • Family sitting and glass sitting room will be used daily.
  • Existing old house can continue serving functions like pooja room and overflow accommodation.

Occasional use

  • Master bedroom and Zain's bedroom on first floor for family visits.
  • Guest rooms on third floor used occasionally.
  • Full family likely uses the house around 60 days/year.
  • Design must prioritize practicality, durability and ease of maintenance.

3. Final Ground Floor Drawing

Latest ground-floor layout note: Front garden → entrance verandah → entrance corridor. Right side starts with glass sitting room with corridor access. Left side has parent bedroom, modern kitchen, traditional kitchen and pantry/store. No wall between modern and traditional kitchen. Stair lobby sits at rear-right with corridor access. Solid wall before family sitting area. Rear verandah and rear garden at the back.

4. First & Third Floor Drawing Reference

Important: First floor should include master bedroom + attached bathroom + dressing, Zain's bedroom with direct attached bathroom, one additional smaller common bathroom, family lounge, balcony and washing area. Third floor should include two guest bedrooms, two attached bathrooms and a large terrace; no living room required on the third floor.

5. Ground Floor – Final Layout Requirements

ZoneRequirements
Kitchen WingModern kitchen placed before/next to the traditional kitchen, with no wall between them. Traditional Himachali kitchen includes chulha and floor utensil washing area. Pantry/store sits after the traditional kitchen and has direct access from kitchen.
Kitchen / Sitting PartitionA solid wall should separate the entrance corridor/kitchen side from the family sitting area. The family sitting area should feel private and not be visible immediately from the entrance.
Sitting AreaSmaller family sitting area, simple furniture, daily-use space. Kitchen should not visually dominate it.
Glass Sitting RoomFirst room on the right side after entering, with direct access from the corridor and front garden view; tea / reading / winter sun room.
Utility BlockRight-side utility sequence: stair lobby at rear-right with corridor access, separate toilet, separate bath area, washing area and utility/store. Toilet and bath must remain separate rooms.
Parent BedroomGround floor parent bedroom on the left side with private access from the entrance corridor; parents can live comfortably without using stairs.
Ground floor zoning:
Kitchen Zone + Pantry → Solid Wall / Arch Partition → Family Sitting → Glass Sitting Room / Verandah / Garden

6. Kitchen Concept – Final

Kitchen design requirements

  • Chulha should be on floor / traditional raised platform level.
  • Remove any central modern island from the kitchen reference.
  • Traditional and modern kitchens should connect directly.
  • Pantry/store must sit beside the kitchen and should have a direct door/opening from the kitchen itself.
  • Keep only the floor-level utensil washing area for traditional use; no separate low sitting cleaning platform required.
  • Add standing sink in modern support kitchen.
  • Kitchen zone should be enclosed from sitting area using a solid partition or large arch opening.
Latest approved drawing note: Keep the ground floor as shown in the final plan image: no dining table, family sitting only; traditional and modern kitchen as one zone; floor utensil washing area only; pantry/store with direct entrance from kitchen; solid wall after pantry; parent bedroom closer to kitchen; washing/toilet/bath moved to service end.

7. First & Third Floor Requirements

First floor – family floor

  • Master bedroom with attached bathroom and dressing.
  • Zain's bedroom with attached bathroom and direct access from bedroom.
  • Additional smaller common bathroom.
  • Family lounge / seating area.
  • Balcony with mountain views.
  • Independent washing area.

Third floor – guest floor

  • Guest bedroom 1 with attached bathroom.
  • Guest bedroom 2 with attached bathroom.
  • Large open terrace occupying approx. 40–50% of floor plate.
  • No living room on this floor.
  • Terrace prioritized over enclosed space.

8. Room Direction Images

9. Windows, Flooring & Materials

Principle: Lots of light, not lots of glass. Use low-maintenance materials suitable for Himachal climate.

10. What To Avoid

Avoid

  • Old chalet-style design references.
  • Excessive glazing or glass walls.
  • Double-height glass façades.
  • Large centre island in the modern kitchen.
  • Separate low sitting cleaning platform in addition to floor utensil washing area.
  • Western fireplace instead of traditional chulha.
  • Fully open kitchen overlooking sitting room; solid wall should come after pantry.
  • Toilet and bath connected through each other.
  • Large carport / parking frontage as main visual identity.

Do

  • Design from kitchen and parents' daily use first.
  • Keep three floors clearly visible.
  • Use partial third floor with terrace.
  • Keep pantry beside kitchen.
  • Separate kitchen and sitting with solid partition / arch.
  • Keep house timeless, practical and low maintenance.

11. Architect Deliverables Required

Concept stage

  • Updated 3-level exterior elevation based on cream villa reference.
  • Ground floor plan reflecting final kitchen, partition, pantry and utility changes.
  • First floor with Zain bathroom direct bedroom access.
  • Third floor with 2 guest rooms + terrace.
  • Material palette and rough cost direction.

Detailed stage

  • Structural drawings.
  • Electrical / plumbing layout.
  • Window and door schedule.
  • Kitchen and bathroom drawings.
  • BOQ estimate within ₹75 lakh–₹1 crore target.
Prepared for architect discussion – Patta Barawari Family Home
